title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — June 2026 (v3.1 EIA-Validated Methodology)
methodology: Two-stage aggregation using exact BLS 2024-2025 relative importance weights. Stage 1: component-level forecasts. Stage 2: weighted aggregation. Verification: Food+Energy+Core=100.000% and Shelter+CoreGoods+CoreServices=80.557%. Energy components validated with AAA retail gasoline ($3.884/gal Jul 10) and EIA retail ($3.777/gal Jul 6). WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%). Backtest: 15 months weight accuracy MAE 0.035% core, 0.146% headline.
summary: June 2026 CPI headline +0.1% m/m (prior +0.5%) as gasoline collapse drags energy sharply lower. Core +0.2% m/m (prior +0.2%) with sticky shelter and services offsetting soft core goods. AAA retail gasoline $3.884/gal Jul 10 vs May avg ~$4.45 (-12.7%). Energy CPI deflation ≈ -2.0% m/m composite, subtracting ~0.12ppt from headline. June jobs miss (+57K vs +120K exp) and Warsh's first FOMC (Jun 16-17 hawkish hold) cap hawkish tail risk. 10Y UST 4.56%. VIX 15.82. Market reaction likely risk-on if core stays contained.
baseCase: Cooler headline on energy drag; core inline at +0.2% with shelter sticky and services stable. Weak jobs data caps hawkish reaction.

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: AAA retail gasoline $3.884/gal Jul 10 vs May avg ~$4.45 (-12.7%). EIA retail $3.777/gal Jul 6. Energy CPI deflation ≈ -2.0% m/m composite, subtracting ~0.12ppt from headline. Core stable (+0.2%) with shelter sticky (+0.3%) and core services firm (+0.25%) offsetting soft core goods (0.0%). June jobs miss (+57K) shifts Fed focus to growth risks. Warsh's first FOMC (Jun 16-17) removed easing bias, dot plot shows one hike in 2026. 10Y 4.56% anchors rate sensitivity. VIX 15.82 signals complacency.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.1% m/m, Core +0.2% m/m — gasoline collapse dominates headline; core stable with shelter sticky and services firm
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields 4.45-4.55%, DXY 100-101. Tech ($QQQ) and duration ($TLT) supported if core does not re-accelerate. Airlines ($UAL) and homebuilders ($DHI) benefit from energy/mortgage relief. Energy sector ($XLE) underperforms.
scenarios.invalidation: Core services re-accelerate above +0.35% m/m; shelter prints above +0.40%; gasoline rebound on Iran flare-up pushes energy CPI positive.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% m/m, Core +0.3% m/m — core services re-accelerate despite gasoline drop; Iran ceasefire collapse limits energy downside
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields spike 4.60%+, DXY 101+. Tech selloff ($NVDA, $MSFT). Gold ($GLD) squeeze. USD long. $SPY PUT setups. $VIX CALL. $XLE short-cover rally.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by one-off transportation component; core goods actually deflationary; gasoline continues to fall through July.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% m/m, Core +0.1% m/m — oil collapse fully captured, shelter decelerates faster than expected, core goods mildly deflationary
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields drop 4.40-4.50%, DXY 99-100. Aggressive risk-on: $QQQ +2-3%, $UAL +8%, $DHI +5%. $TLT strong. Fed-cut odds rise. $XLE -5%.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced benign outcome after weak jobs; Warsh 'price stability' rhetoric prevents rally; Iran ceasefire breaks down.

setups.bias: Long duration on cooler or inline core
setups.trigger: Core CPI <= +0.2% and 10Y yield fails 4.55%; DXY below 101
setups.invalidation: Core services above +0.30% with 10Y yield breaks 4.65%.

setups.bias: Long on soft headline — rate-cut pricing revived
setups.invalidation: Hot core with 10Y yield above 4.60%; DXY above 102.

setups.bias: Long — jet fuel collapse + summer demand intact
setups.trigger: AAA gasoline <$3.90 and CPI headline soft; summer bookings strong
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI above $80, or travel demand disappoints.

setups.bias: Long — mortgage rate relief on soft CPI + 10Y fade
setups.trigger: 10Y yield below 4.50% and core CPI inline or cooler
setups.invalidation: 10Y yields spike above 4.65% on hot CPI or PPI passthrough.

setups.bias: Short — oil supply recovering, Iran ceasefire holding, gasoline in free fall
setups.trigger: WTI below $75 and gasoline continues to decline; API inventories build
setups.invalidation: Iran ceasefire collapses, Strait of Hormuz disruption, or OPEC+ emergency cuts.

setups.bias: Long hedge — Fed uncertainty + weak jobs + geopolitical risk
setups.trigger: DXY below 100 and real yields compress; geopolitical flare-up
setups.invalidation: DXY above 102 with 10Y yields >4.70%; strong risk-on kills safe-haven bid.

watchItems.label: Fed path / Warsh
watchItems.value: 1 hike in 2026 (dot plot 3.8%) — hawkish hold at Jun 16-17 FOMC
watchItems.note: Warsh's first meeting. Removed easing bias from statement. 9/18 participants expect hike. June NFP +57K cuts hike odds. PCE forecasts 3.6% headline, 3.3% core.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Key regime line. Below 4.50% = growth rally. Above 4.65% = risk-off. Real yields +10bp 30d; breakevens -11bp = market pricing disinflation + tight money.

watchItems.label: AAA Retail Gasoline
watchItems.note: AAA National Average. Down from $4.151 month ago (-6.4%). Down from $4.56 May 21 peak. Reversed +5 cents Jul 9 on Iran ceasefire uncertainty. EIA retail $3.777 Jul 6 is primary input.

watchItems.label: ISM Prices Paid
watchItems.note: Manufacturing prices cooling from 82.1 peak. Services prices sticky at 71.3. Watch Jun services print.

watchItems.label: DXY
watchItems.note: Mid-range. Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough. YTD +2.70%.

watchItems.label: Manheim Used Vehicle Index
watchItems.note: Used car prices firm but seasonal depreciation starting. Full Jun release Jul 8.

watchItems.label: WTI Crude
watchItems.note: Bounced from $69.60 Jul 6 after Jul 7 Strait of Hormuz tanker strike. Iran ceasefire uncertainty lingers. Directional only — NOT used for gasoline CPI forecast.

watchItems.label: VIX
watchItems.note: Low VIX = complacency risk. Post-FOMC normalization. CPI day could see 18-22 spike on surprise. Jul 8 hit 18.91 on Iran flare-up.

watchItems.label: June Jobs
watchItems.note: Big miss vs +120K expected. 74K downward revision to Apr/May. Cuts hawkish tail risk. Fed may shift to growth-monitoring mode.

keyDrivers: Oil/gasoline collapse: AAA retail gasoline $3.884/gal Jul 10 vs May avg ~$4.45 (-12.7%). Energy CPI deflation ≈ -2.0% m/m composite, subtracting ~0.12ppt from headline. EIA retail $3.777 Jul 6. WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%).
June jobs miss: +57K NFP vs +120K expected. 74K downward revision. UR 4.2%. Shifts Fed focus to growth risks, reducing hawkish tail risk for CPI reaction. Sep hike odds dropped.

Shelter stickiness: Zillow/Apartment List leading indicators slowing but BLS 6-12 month lag means OER still prints ~0.3% m/m. Rent of primary residence ~2.5% y/y. Full deceleration not yet in BLS data.
DXY 100.87: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets any PPI passthrough. Core goods may see mild deflation (0.0% m/m).

Warsh's first FOMC (Jun 16-17): Hawkish hold. Removed easing bias. Dot plot shows one hike in 2026. Market pricing tighter real money and rapid disinflation simultaneously (10Y flat 30d, real yields +10bp, breakevens -11bp).
Core PPI passthrough watch: Prior hot PPI prints feed into core CPI with 1-3 month lag. If services passthrough accelerates in Jul/Aug, core undershoot narrative breaks.
risks: Core PPI passthrough: Hot PPI feeds into core CPI with 1-3 month lag. If services passthrough materializes in July, core surprise to upside.
Iran ceasefire collapse: Jul 7 Strait of Hormuz tanker strike already bounced WTI from $69.60 to $73.52. Full collapse = WTI $80+ = CPI revision to hotter.
FOMC hawkish limits reaction: Warsh 'price stability' rhetoric + dot plot hike = unpredictable Fed. Market may 'sell the rally' even on soft CPI.
ISM Services Prices re-acceleration: Jun print if >75 = core services sticky confirmation.
Demand weakness: Global recession fears rising. If oil and risk assets both fall, soft CPI = bad news for equities (stagflation fear).
Shelter lag uncertainty: Zillow/Apartment List slowing but BLS 6-12 month lag. Faster deceleration = core downside surprise; slower = core sticky.
